title: Rapid Ribosome (Polysome) Profiling
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"65aou","text":"Ribosome profiling is a powerful technique used to study translation at a genome-wide level. It involves the sequencing of ribosome-protected mRNA fragments to determine the positions of ribosomes on transcripts. This information can be used to infer translation rates and identify translated open reading frames. While traditional ribosome profiling methods can be time-consuming and expensive, our method is rapid and cost-effective, and the inclusion of UMIs allows for precise quantification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 1 µL of 100 micromolar (µM) RiboS_linker (linker RNA oligo) to 10 µL of RNA, denature it for 00:01:00 at 70 °C , and then cool it to Room temperature

Set up the ligation reaction below and incubate for 01:30:00 at 37 °C A B 1 Component Volume (μl) 2 RNA and linker 11 3 RtcB Reaction Buffer (10X) 2 4 SUPERase·In (20 U/μl) 1 5 1 mM GTP 2 6 10 mM MnCl2 2 7 RtcB RNA Ligase 1.5 8 H20 0

%s %s Mix thoroughly by gently pipetting up and down at least 10 times, then centrifuge briefly to collect the solution to the bottom of the tube Denature for 00:05:00 at 70 °C in a thermal cycler and then place on ice for ≥ 00:01:00

Vortex the Template Switching RT Buffer briefly followed by a quick spin Combine the following components in a reaction tube %s %s Mix thoroughly by gently pipetting up and down at least 10 times, then centrifuge briefly to collect the solution to the bottom of the tube

Combine 7 µL RT reaction mix (above) with 13 µL of the annealed mix, mix well by gently pipetting up and down at least 10 times, then centrifuge briefly to collect the solution to the bottom of the tube Incubate as below A B 1 Temp (C) Time (m) 2 42 90 3 85 5 4 4 1

Add 90 µL AMPure XP beads to the PCR product Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 Place on a magnetic rack Aspirate supernatant Add 200 µL 70 % (v/v) ethanol Wait for 00:00:30 Aspirate and discard the supernatant Add 200 µL 70 % (v/v) ethanol Wait for 00:00:30 Aspirate and discard the supernatant Resuspend beads in 10 µL of H20 Incubate for 00:02:00 Transfer to a clean PCR tube
Optional but recommended preselection of small RNA fragments using either a urea PAGE gel or bead and membrane selection.
Perform RNA isolation and nuclease footprinting as appropriate for your cell type (see steps 1-17 here for cultured mammalian cells)
Run a 1% agarose gel, excise the desired band, and perform a gel cleanup.
To perform DASH to deplete unwanted sequences (e.g., rRNA) follow the protocol here .

Run a tapestation or bioanalyzer chip Do qubit or qPCR using an Illumina library quant kit to quantify the library and pool the samples (see Illumina Sequencing Coverage Calculator for pooling information)
10 µL RNA plus 30 µL RNA XP beads plus 10 µL 100% isopropanol
Transfer the excised gel slice to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and weigh it.
Add 4 volumes of Monarch RNA Cleanup Binding Buffer to the tube with the slice
Incubate the sample between 55 °C , gently mixing periodically until the gel slice is completely dissolved (generally < 00:10:00 )
Add two volumes of absolute ethanol to the sample and mix well by pipetting up and down
Incubate the sample between 55 °C or an additional 00:05:00
Insert an RNA cleanup column into a collection tube, load the sample onto the column and close the cap
Re-insert the column into the collection tube
Transfer the column to a clean 1.5 ml microfuge tube
Add 10 µL nucleae-free water directly to the membrane and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Spin for 00:01:00
Spin for 00:01:00 , then discard flow-through
Add 500 µL RNA Cleanup Wash Buffer
Spin for 00:01:00 , then discard flow-through
go to step # for a total of two washes.
Add the appropriate volume of 2X RNA loading buffer to each RNA sample
Denature the samples for 80 °C 00:01:00 followed by ≥ 00:02:00 on On ice
Load the samples on the polyacrylamide gel with TBE running buffer
Separate by electrophoresis for 01:30:00 at 100V
Stain the gel for 00:03:00 with 1X SYBR Gold in 1X TBE running buffer on a shaker
Visualize the gel and excise desired region (20-40nts)
Add 100 µL RNA Cleanup Binding Buffer

go to step # for a total of two washes.

Add 10 µL nucleae-free water directly to the membrane and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
